allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps combinatorial-effects trace how multiple interacting forces amplify or offset one another across a larger whole.
allies.source_quote: multiple factors or forces, when aligned and interacting, produce an outcome that is disproportionately larger

allies.rationale_text: Second-order-thinking helps combinatorial-effects track the downstream dominoes created when several forces compound at once.
allies.source_quote: seeing the relatedness and the effects from the relatedness

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks combinatorial-effects when teams defend a favorite single-cause story instead of following the interacting drivers.
antagonists.source_quote: motivated reasoning prevents honest adaptation to reality

structured_tensions.tension_text: Combinatorial-effects conflicts with simplification when pursuit of multi-factor interaction effects prevents identification of the single dominant variable that explains most of the variance.
structured_tensions.source_quote: combinatorial-effects conflicts with simplification (simplification) when pursuit of multi-factor interaction effects prevents identification of the single dominant variable that explains most of the variance.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Combinatorial-effects conflicts with occams-razor when combinatorial explanations are preferred over simpler models that predict equally well with fewer interacting terms.
structured_tensions.source_quote: combinatorial-effects conflicts with occams-razor (Occam's razor) when combinatorial explanations are preferred over simpler models that predict equally well with fewer interacting terms.

curation_notes.summary: Relation surface stays inside the current graph vocabulary and keeps omission stronger than weak forcing.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 3 target ids or import donor-shaped relation neighborhoods to rescue weak local grounding.
open_questions: Some source-real neighbors remain omitted because the current graph vocabulary would force weaker mappings than this conservative pass allows.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only and is noted only in batch artifacts.
antagonists: No multihop or higher-order semantics were introduced in these Wave 3 files.
structured_tensions: Explicit tensions were preserved only where the raw source clearly earned a current-vocabulary target.
